steps.title: Identify what comes before and after the blank
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses: (1) “the Puerto Rican municipality of Manatí is well known for its history as a cultural center in the early 20th [blank]” and (2) “this distinction has earned it the fitting nickname of ‘the Athens of Puerto Rico.’” A punctuation mark must correctly join these two complete thoughts.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — century and: Uses a coordinating conjunction without a comma before it — violates the rule that a comma must precede a coordinating conjunction joining two independent clauses (comma + conjunction rule). ✗
  • Choice B — century: Places two independent clauses side by side with no punctuation — creates a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — century,: Uses only a comma to join two independent clauses — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — century;: A semicolon correctly joins two independent clauses without a conjunction. ✓
That is the correct answer.
